title: Calamintha hederacea
intro: Calamintha hederacea, also known as creeping calmer or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herbaceous perennial plant in the mint family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its carminative and antispasmodic properties, which are believed to provide relief from digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Calamintha hederacea include essential oils, including thymol, borneol, and bornyl acetate, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calamintha hederacea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to calm the digestive system and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Calamintha hederacea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Calamintha and is a perennial herb. The species name hederacea means 'ivy-leaved', referring to the plant's characteristic leaf shape and growth habit. Calamintha hederacea is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found in woodland edges, hedgerows, and scrubland. It is a relatively small plant, typically growing to a height of around 10-30 cm.
uses: Calamintha hederacea, also known as creeping savory, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. It is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help with wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant's leaves and stems can be used to make a tea that may aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Additionally, it is thought to have a calming effect, which can help with anxiety and insomnia. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these medicinal uses.
benefits: Calamintha hederacea, commonly known as creeping mint or Nepeta hederacea, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The essential oils in Calamintha hederacea have been identified to include borneol, camphene, and limonene, among others. Flavonoids present in this plant have been found to include flavonoid glycosides and phenolic acids.
